news.headline: Iran’s Soccer Team Allowed Into U.S. for World Cup, but Many Staff Denied
news.summary: After months of doubts over their participation, Iran’s players have received visas for the United States just days before the World Cup begins.

news.headline: A Sherpa Survived 6 Days Alone on Everest. His Family Says He Was Abandoned.
news.summary: Dawa Sherpa, 57, was found alive on Thursday, nearly a week after he was last seen on the mountain. His wife says more could have been done to find him sooner.

news.headline: I wanted to quit Eurovision twice - then won it, says Bangaranga singer Dara
news.summary: The Bulgarian singer says her historic victory in Vienna came close to never happening.

news.headline: Palestinian baby killed by Israeli gunfire in West Bank, health ministry says
news.summary: The Israeli military says Friday's "incident is under review", expressing "deep sorrow for any harm caused".

news.headline: Pamela Hicks, Lady-in-Waiting to Elizabeth II of Britain, Dies at 97
news.summary: The queen’s third cousin, she was a bridesmaid at the royal wedding in 1947, and witnessed firsthand pivotal moments in British history.

news.headline: Fall in official Ebola numbers appears to be good news but it's not that simple
news.summary: There are now 380 confirmed cases of Ebola in DR Congo, far lower than initial estimates of suspected cases, writes Fergus Walsh.
